mechanism_narrative: A persistent downward trend in the federal funds rate over the trailing three months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other regime conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or mean forward return in declining-rate months is not significantly greater than in flat/rising-rate months (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month span, it signals an accelerating labor market deterioration that leads investors to price in recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter (60 trading days) until the unemployment trend stabilizes.
failure_condition: In periods where the UNRATE trigger fires, forward 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the average triggered return is not statistically distinguishable from the unconditional average (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) growth and payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth signals synchronized labor-income-driven consumption strength that jointly supports corporate earnings expectations; the interaction of both accelerating together produces stronger positive forward S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days than either series alone.
failure_condition: The joint interaction term (RSAFS growth x PAYEMS growth) shows no incremental predictive power over either single-series model, measured by no improvement in out-of-sample Rank IC of at least 0.02, across a 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% while CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation remains elevated above 4%, the market interprets the Fed as locked into a restrictive stance, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; this effect is specific to high-rate, high-inflation regimes and reverses once inflation decelerates.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with high inflation
failure_condition: During periods when both trigger conditions hold, forward 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or mean triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in industrial production (INDPRO) over the trailing month reflects expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue capacity, translating into modest positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as short-term momentum traders react to improving supply-side fundamentals.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month INDPRO change and forward 5-day SPX returns is below 0.02 across any rolling 2-year window, indicating no reliable short-horizon signal.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) of more than 10% from its trailing 6-month average signals weakening consumer demand for durable, interest-rate-sensitive spending, foreshadowing broader economic slowdown and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns following a triggered HOUST decline are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the triggered mean return does not differ significantly from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising FEDFUNDS alongside rising UNRATE indicates the Fed is tightening into a weakening labor market, a historically dangerous policy-error configuration that amplifies recession risk pricing and produces sharply negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter (60 trading days), stronger than either signal alone.
regime_hint: policy tightening amid labor market deterioration
failure_condition: The interaction model (FEDFUNDS trend x UNRATE trend) fails to outperform the additive single-series baseline by at least 0.02 Rank IC in out-of-sample testing over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a deceleration in month-over-month PAYEMS growth below its trailing 12-month average signals an approaching labor market inflection that short-horizon traders quickly price into equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days; this effect is muted or absent in early/mid-cycle regimes where slower job growth is interpreted benignly.
failure_condition: In late-cycle periods, forward 5-day SPX returns following PAYEMS deceleration are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect is statistically indistinguishable (p > 0.05) from the effect observed in early/mid-cycle periods, indicating no regime specificity.
